title: Go Essentials
description: A practical introduction to the Go programming language. You will learn Go's type system, functions, structs, interfaces, error handling, and concurrency primitives. The course emphasizes Go's strengths in building HTTP servers, CLI tools, and concurrent programs, culminating in a real-world project.

modules.title: Go Setup and Hello World
modules.description: Install Go, configure your workspace, understand GOPATH vs modules, and write your first program.
objectives: Install Go and verify the installation
Initialize a project with go mod init
Write, build, and run a Hello World program
Understand Go's compilation model

modules.title: Types, Variables, and Constants
modules.description: Learn Go's type system: basic types, zero values, type inference with :=, constants, and iota.
objectives: Declare variables with var and short declaration :=
Use basic types: int, float64, string, bool
Define constants and use iota for enumerations
Explain zero values for each type

modules.title: Control Flow and Functions
modules.description: Master if/else, switch, for loops, and function definitions including multiple return values and named returns.
objectives: Write functions with multiple return values
Use for as the only loop construct in Go
Apply switch statements with and without a condition
Understand defer and its execution order

modules.title: Arrays, Slices, and Maps
modules.description: Work with Go's composite types. Understand the difference between arrays and slices, and use maps for key-value data.
objectives: Create and manipulate slices with append, copy, and slicing
Understand slice internals: length, capacity, and underlying arrays
Use maps for key-value storage and check for key existence
Iterate over slices and maps with range

modules.title: Structs and Methods
modules.description: Define custom types with structs, attach methods with value and pointer receivers, and compose types with embedding.
objectives: Define structs and initialize them with literals
Implement methods with value and pointer receivers
Use struct embedding for composition
Understand when to use pointer vs value receivers

modules.title: Interfaces
modules.description: Understand Go's implicit interface satisfaction. Design with interfaces, use the empty interface, and apply type assertions and type switches.
objectives: Define interfaces and implement them implicitly
Use common standard library interfaces (io.Reader, io.Writer, fmt.Stringer)
Apply type assertions and type switches
Design functions that accept interfaces for flexibility

modules.title: Error Handling
modules.description: Handle errors the Go way with the error interface, custom error types, errors.Is/As, and wrapping with fmt.Errorf.
objectives: Return and check errors idiomatically
Create custom error types implementing the error interface
Wrap errors with fmt.Errorf and %w
Inspect error chains with errors.Is and errors.As

modules.title: Goroutines and Concurrency
modules.description: Launch concurrent goroutines and coordinate them with WaitGroups. Understand Go's concurrency philosophy: 'share memory by communicating.'
objectives: Launch goroutines with the go keyword
Synchronize goroutines with sync.WaitGroup
Protect shared data with sync.Mutex
Explain the difference between concurrency and parallelism

modules.title: Channels and Select
modules.description: Communicate between goroutines using channels. Use buffered channels, directional channels, and the select statement for multiplexing.
objectives: Create and use unbuffered and buffered channels
Apply directional channel types for safety
Multiplex channel operations with select
Implement common patterns: fan-out, fan-in, and pipeline

modules.title: Packages and Modules
modules.description: Organize code into packages, manage dependencies with Go modules, and understand visibility rules with exported/unexported identifiers.
objectives: Structure a multi-package project
Manage dependencies with go mod tidy and go.sum
Understand exported vs unexported identifiers
Use internal packages for encapsulation

modules.title: Testing in Go
modules.description: Write tests, benchmarks, and examples using Go's built-in testing package. Use table-driven tests and understand test coverage.
objectives: Write test functions with the testing package
Use table-driven tests for comprehensive coverage
Run benchmarks and interpret results
Generate and analyze test coverage reports

modules.title: JSON Handling and HTTP Servers
modules.description: Marshal and unmarshal JSON with struct tags. Build HTTP servers using net/http with handlers, middleware, and routing.
objectives: Marshal and unmarshal JSON with encoding/json and struct tags
Create HTTP handlers with http.HandlerFunc
Build a REST API with routing and middleware
Make HTTP client requests with http.Client

modules.title: Capstone: Build a CLI Tool
modules.description: Build a complete CLI application using cobra or the flag package, combining file I/O, JSON processing, error handling, and concurrency.
objectives: Design a CLI tool with subcommands and flags
Process files and data concurrently with goroutines
Handle errors gracefully with user-friendly messages
Write tests for core functionality
